clusters.label: self-evo-godel
clusters.title: 自进化与哥德尔智能体

self-evo-godel.title: Gödel Agents for Verifiable Self-Improvement
self-evo-godel.abstract: A framework for agents that propose, verify, and safely adopt self-modifications through proof-carrying improvement loops.

self-evo-godel.title: Recursive Improvement Under Bounded Verification Budgets
self-evo-godel.abstract: Studies how recursive agents decide which internal changes are worth verification when proofs and experiments are both costly.

self-evo-godel.title: Self-Evolving Tool Policies for Long Horizon Agents
self-evo-godel.abstract: Introduces a tool-policy evolution loop that can preserve task reliability while adapting to new external APIs.

self-evo-godel.title: Darwinian Program Search for Agent Self-Revision
self-evo-godel.abstract: Combines evolutionary search and agent traces to discover small self-revisions that improve delivery quality.

self-evo-godel.title: Proof-Guided Patch Acceptance for AI Assistants
self-evo-godel.abstract: A patch acceptance protocol for assistants that requires executable evidence before changing their own workflow.

self-evo-godel.title: Meta-Controllers for Self-Improving Agent Fleets
self-evo-godel.abstract: Explores fleet-level meta-controllers that decide which agent improvements should be promoted globally.

agent-os-harness.title: An Agentic OS for Persistent Project Workspaces
agent-os-harness.abstract: Presents an operating layer for persistent agent workspaces, permissioned tools, and resumable project context.

agent-os-harness.title: Harness Contracts for Multi-Tool Language Agents
agent-os-harness.abstract: Defines harness contracts that make tool execution observable, reversible, and auditable in production agents.

agent-os-harness.title: Capability Sandboxes for Autonomous Workflows
agent-os-harness.abstract: A sandboxing approach for autonomous agents that separates planning authority from destructive execution.

agent-os-harness.title: Tracing and Replay for Agent Runtime Debugging
agent-os-harness.abstract: Records agent actions as replayable traces so workflow bugs can be reproduced across models.

agent-os-harness.title: Stateful Tool Routers for Long-Lived Agents
agent-os-harness.abstract: Shows that stateful tool routing can reduce repeated context loading in long-lived assistant sessions.

agent-os-harness.title: Permission Lattices for Agent Workbenches
agent-os-harness.abstract: Models agent permissions as a lattice that can be safely narrowed or expanded by human operators.

multi-agent-research.title: Research Swarms for Hypothesis Generation and Refutation
multi-agent-research.abstract: Coordinates specialist agents to propose, test, and refute scientific hypotheses with shared evidence boards.

multi-agent-research.title: Experiment Schedulers for GPU-Backed Agent Labs
multi-agent-research.abstract: A scheduler that lets research agents allocate GPU experiments under budget, priority, and dependency constraints.

multi-agent-research.title: Blackboard Coordination for Deep Research Agents
multi-agent-research.abstract: Uses a shared blackboard to coordinate web search, paper reading, experiment planning, and report writing agents.

multi-agent-research.title: Agent Peer Review for Automated Literature Surveys
multi-agent-research.abstract: Evaluates peer-review loops between reading agents to reduce omissions in literature surveys.

multi-agent-research.title: Benchmarking Collaborative Scientific Agents
multi-agent-research.abstract: A benchmark suite for measuring whether scientific agents can collaborate without losing provenance.

multi-agent-research.title: Evidence Graphs for Agentic Research Pipelines
multi-agent-research.abstract: Represents claims, papers, experiments, and counterexamples as an evidence graph for research agents.

memory-reflection.title: Long-Term Memory Compaction for Project Agents
memory-reflection.abstract: A memory compaction method that preserves project decisions, failed attempts, and reusable context across sessions.

memory-reflection.title: Reflection Buffers for Self-Correcting Agents
memory-reflection.abstract: Reflection buffers help agents identify repeated failure modes and change future plans without overfitting.

memory-reflection.title: Meta-Learned Retrieval Policies for Agent Memory
memory-reflection.abstract: Learns retrieval policies that select memories by task phase instead of simple semantic similarity.

memory-reflection.title: Session Handoff Summaries for Human-Agent Teams
memory-reflection.abstract: Creates compact handoff summaries that let agents and humans resume interrupted project work.

memory-reflection.title: Counterfactual Reflection for Planning Agents
memory-reflection.abstract: Uses counterfactual traces to teach planning agents when a different tool sequence would have succeeded.

memory-reflection.title: Memory Provenance in Multi-Session Assistants
memory-reflection.abstract: Tracks memory provenance to prevent outdated project assumptions from silently contaminating new sessions.

safety-eval.title: Feedback-Aware Exclusion Policies for Research Agents
safety-eval.abstract: Learns exclusion policies from user feedback so research agents stop resurfacing low-value paper families.

safety-eval.title: Evaluating Autonomous Agent Changes Before Deployment
safety-eval.abstract: Proposes pre-deployment evaluations for autonomous changes to tools, prompts, memories, and workflows.

safety-eval.title: Human Feedback Queues for Agent Research Triage
safety-eval.abstract: A triage queue that converts sparse human feedback into future priority scores for paper recommendation.

safety-eval.title: Risk-Aware Ranking for Autonomous Literature Monitoring
safety-eval.abstract: Ranks papers by potential product relevance and risk, using separate signals for novelty and operational impact.

safety-eval.title: Confidence Calibration for Agent-Curated Research Feeds
safety-eval.abstract: Calibrates confidence estimates attached to research feed items so humans can prioritize review effort.

safety-eval.title: Red-Team Signals for Self-Improving Agent Pipelines
safety-eval.abstract: Injects red-team signals into self-improvement pipelines to detect unsafe optimization shortcuts.
